title: Subscriber Churn Prediction from Usage Pattern Decay
dataSources: `index=subscriber` with `sourcetype=subscriber:usage` (weekly or daily BSS/CRM aggregates per subscriber) and optional `sourcetype=subscriber:recharge` for prepaid top-up streams. **Index patterns**: separate **PII-heavy** subscriber index with strict RBAC (`subscriber_id` tokenised where possible); consider **pseudo-IDs** for Splunk analysts. **Mechanisms**: **DB Connect** scheduled extracts from BSS usage marts (`data_mb`, `voice_min`, `sms_count`) and CRM contract fields (`plan_type`, `contract_end_date`); optional CSV monitor from campaign exports. **Key fields**: `subscriber_id`, `data_mb`, `voice_min`, `sms_count`, `recharge_amount`, `plan_type`, `contract_end_date`, `dest_number` (optional social reach proxy). **Volume**: aggregate usage feeds are lighter than rated CDR — commonly **500k–5M subscriber-day rows/day** for national MNO depending on rollup grain. **Parsing notes**: harmonise **rolling 7-day** boundaries to billing timezone; exclude **zero-balance dormant** IMSIs via eligibility flag; clamp outliers from metering glitches (`data_mb > 10*rolling_p99`).
app: **Splunk DB Connect 3.x** against BSS data warehouse facts (`subscriber_usage_daily`) — preferred for freshness and keyed joins to CRM dimensions. Alternative: **CSV monitor** for offline usage extracts from Hadoop/Snowflake offload. Prepaid recharge feeds via **`subscriber:recharge`** monitor or JDBC **incremental**. Integrate consent flags from CRM (**GDPR**/CPRA) as lookup to suppress opted-out subscribers from scoring.
spl:
index=subscriber sourcetype=subscriber:usage
| bin _time span=7d
| stats sum(data_mb) as weekly_data, sum(voice_min) as weekly_voice, dc(dest_number) as unique_contacts by subscriber_id, _time
| streamstats window=4 avg(weekly_data) as avg_data, avg(weekly_voice) as avg_voice by subscriber_id
| eval data_decay=round(((avg_data-weekly_data)/avg_data)*100, 1), voice_decay=round(((avg_voice-weekly_voice)/avg_voice)*100, 1)
| where data_decay > 40 OR voice_decay > 50
| sort -data_decay
description: Tracks each subscriber's weekly voice minutes, mobile data megabytes, and social reach proxies across rolling four-week windows, flagging steep drops versus their own trailing averages before port-out behaviors fully appear.
value: Marketing and retention can intervene with offers or save teams while the subscriber is still reachable, reducing involuntary churn and protecting recurring service revenue without waiting for disconnect or port requests.
implementation: Backfill twelve weeks of usage for baseline stability; exclude machine-to-machine identifiers; enrich with segment or contract type for prioritized outreach; tune decay percentages separately for prepaid versus postpaid.
visualization: **Row 1 — KPI tiles**: count subscribers over decay thresholds, segmented prepaid vs postpaid, average `data_decay` / `voice_decay`. **Row 2 — Trend**: timechart of flagged subscribers created weekly. **Row 3 — Detail table**: `subscriber_id`, `data_decay`, `voice_decay`, `plan_type`, `contract_end_date` with drilldown to 8-week sparkline panels. **Row 4 — Supplementary**: scatter `data_decay` vs `voice_decay` coloured by `plan_type`; optional funnel chart of outreach outcomes exported to campaign tool.

references.title: Splunk Lantern — Use Case Explorer: Communications
3GPP TS 32.297 — CDR File Format and Transfer
GSMA TAP3 Standard — Transferred Account Procedure
ITU-T E.425 — IP Network Performance Objectives

knownFalsePositives: **(1) Seasonal travel & roaming reversions** — subscribers who recently returned home show steep drops versus roaming-heavy trailing weeks despite no churn intent; mitigate with roaming flags from mediation or abbreviated suppress windows after roam end. **(2) Planned life-cycle pauses** — medical leave, parental leave, caregiver plans, bereavement freezes; suppression via CRM/BSS paused-service rosters keyed by subscriber. **(3) SIM/device migrations** — SIM swaps or handset replacements create metering blind spots lasting days; join equipment-change feeds and freeze decay scoring for ≥14 days with tunable SLA. **(4) Caps, throttling, fair-usage policies** — once quotas bite, lawful traffic shaping mimics abandonment; segmented thresholds for capped and unlimited plans plus plan-change calendars. **(5) Educational / dorm Wi-Fi offload** — students shift usage wholesale to unmanaged Wi-Fi; optional Wi-Fi offload metrics or institutional calendar suppress periods. **(6) Multi-IMSI households** — wearables/eSIM companions split billed usage across pseudo-identifiers unless household linkage tables unify views. **(7) Collections or fraud bars** — involuntary freezes suppress usage without churn predictive value; BSS **exception/disposition** codes should dampen outbound marketing triggers. **(8) Fibre substitution or broadband migration bundles** deliberately move consumption off mobile for happy customers — cross-check fixed-line KPIs before save-team outreach to avoid irritated “already satisfied” subscribers. **(9) OSS/BSS metering bugs or partial rating outages** flatten usage fleet-wide—not behavioural decay; correlate with outage tickets and mediation health indexes before alerting. **(10) Prepaid dormant accounts with long silent periods** resurrect mid-window causing unstable `streamstats` denominators — require minimum nonzero sample weeks (`where sample_count>=3`) prior to alerting.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Continuous **usage summaries** keyed `subscriber_id` with trustworthy `data_mb`, `voice_min`, optional `sms_count`, optional `dest_number` cardinality proxy.
• **Policy/consent**: legal approval for behavioural scoring exports; Splunk masked IDs if required.
• **DB Connect** or secured batch files from BSS/CRM; roles restrict `subscriber` index.
• Twelve-week historical backfill for stable **trail averages**.
• Exclude M2M/IoT via `device_segment` lookup to avoid mechanical churn signals.
• License modest relative to raw CDR — still plan **growth** when moving from weekly to daily grain.

Step 1 — Configure data collection
**DB Connect example pattern** (pseudo-SQL — adapt schema):

```
SELECT subscriber_id,
       usage_date,
       SUM(data_mb) AS data_mb,
       SUM(voice_min) AS voice_min,
       SUM(sms_count) AS sms_count,
       plan_type,
       contract_end_date
FROM dw.subscriber_usage_daily
WHERE usage_date >= current_date - 56
```

Map output `index=subscriber`, `sourcetype=subscriber:usage`, `_time = usage_date`.

**Recharge (prepaid)**

```ini
[monitor:///opt/bss/recharge/*.csv]
index = subscriber
sourcetype = subscriber:recharge
```

**props.conf**

```ini
[subscriber:usage]
TIME_FORMAT = %Y-%m-%d
MAX_TIMESTAMP_LOOKAHEAD = 10
EVAL-subscriber_id=upper(subscriber_id)
```

**Verification**

```spl
index=subscriber sourcetype=subscriber:usage earliest=-7d
| stats count dc(subscriber_id) avg(data_mb)

index=subscriber subscriber_id="<test_known_active>" earliest=-56d
| timechart span=7d sum(data_mb) sum(voice_min)
```

Step 2 — Create the search and alert

```spl
index=subscriber sourcetype=subscriber:usage subscriber_id!="NOT_SET"
NOT [| inputlookup consent_optout | fields subscriber_id]
| bin _time span=7d aligntime=@w0
| stats sum(data_mb) AS weekly_data, sum(voice_min) AS weekly_voice,
         dc(dest_number) AS unique_contacts BY subscriber_id, _time
| streamstats window=4 avg(weekly_data) AS avg_data avg(weekly_voice) AS avg_voice BY subscriber_id
| eval base_data = if(avg_data=0 OR isnull(avg_data), null(), avg_data)
| eval base_voice = if(avg_voice=0 OR isnull(avg_voice), null(), avg_voice)
| eval data_decay = if(isnull(base_data), null(),
    round(((base_data-weekly_data)/base_data)*100,1))
| eval voice_decay = if(isnull(base_voice), null(),
    round(((base_voice-weekly_voice)/base_voice)*100,1))
| where data_decay > 40 OR voice_decay > 50
| sort - data_decay
```

Understanding this SPL
• **`bin span=7d aligntime=@w0`** aligns weeks consistently (adjust to billing TZ).
• **`stats` weekly** aggregates data/voice/contacts — **contacts** proxies social engagement erosion.
• **`streamstats window=4`** computes **trailing personal baseline** avoiding global averages that bias heavy users vs light users.
• **Zero guards** skip divide-by-null when new subscribers lack history mid-window.
• **Thresholds 40%/50%** are starting points — calibrate prepaid vs postpaid using **prior churn study**.
• **Consent subsearch** enforces ethical guardrails.

Optional prepaid enrichment: run a sibling search against `index=subscriber sourcetype=subscriber:recharge` summarising weekly `sum(recharge_amount)` and reuse the same `streamstats`-based decay thresholds so wallet-funded segments are not blind spots.

```spl
index=subscriber sourcetype=subscriber:recharge earliest=-35d@d
| bin _time span=7d aligntime=@w0
| stats sum(recharge_amount) AS weekly_topup BY subscriber_id, _time
| streamstats window=4 avg(weekly_topup) AS avg_topup BY subscriber_id
| eval topup_decay = if(avg_topup=0 OR isnull(avg_topup), null(),
    round(((avg_topup-weekly_topup)/avg_topup)*100, 1))
| where topup_decay > 40
```

Schedule as Alert: **weekly**, Monday 07:00, range **`-35d@d`**, output to **CSV/lookup** for campaign tools vs paging (avoid alert fatigue — use report).

Step 3 — Validate
(a) Historical cohort holdout — verify decay fired **2–6 weeks** pre-churn for >60% movers.
(b) Inspect **divide-by-null** residual population — should be **negligible** after 4 weeks onboarded.
(c) Compare with **commercial churn model** scores if available — rank correlation sanity.
(d) Drill **100 random non-churn controls** quarterly — false-positive rate KPI.
(e) Segment **prepaid** vs **postpaid** ROC curves.
(f) DPI **QA**: confirm `sms_count` not always zero artefact post BSS upgrade.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Implement four-row dashboard described in **visualization**. Route report to CRM **journey orchestration** daily file drop; throttle campaign touches weekly per subscriber via external cap.
Runbook: (1) Exclude known network outages week. (2) Apply roaming/home travel calendar suppress. (3) Agent script for empathetic outreach. (4) Log outcomes back to Splunk via HEC optionally.

Behavioural churn scoring — statistical rigour and ethical safeguards

Rolling four-week baselines keyed only to subscriber self-history elegantly remove population drift but remain sensitive to tariff migrations that mechanically reduce allowances without churn intent—for example migrating users from unmetered night buckets to taxed buckets exhibits sharp data decay benignly. Enrich SPL with **`plan_migration_events`** lookups keyed by subscriber and effective timestamps to freeze decay scoring for fourteen days following plan change.

Socio-demographic clustering is optional ethically charged enhancement—if Splunk merges external bureau attributes, tighten access controls and minimise retention to campaign windows only. Pseudonymous IDs hashed with rotating salts minimise re-identification while keeping longitudinal analytics consistent within rolling windows.

Operationalising decay alerts as **marketing lists** differs jurisictionally: GDPR legitimate interest assessments, CPRA opt-out mandates, Brazilian LGPD DPIA artefacts should be hyperlink references in onboarding documentation rather than SPL comments. Practical Splunk manifestation: KV store `subscriber_consents` surfaced through subsearch exclusions as demonstrated; refresh hourly from CRM webhook or DB Connect CDC stream.

Variance stabilisation demands **seasonality harmonics**: prepend an optional Fourier feature engineering step (Splunk seldom used for Fourier but `sin(2*pi*(date_week/52))`) as experimental multipliers only after data science validates uplift compared to simplistic thresholds—document as optional appendix search not default alert pipeline.

Cold-start subscribers lacking four weeks remain invisible to base streamstats methodology; bifurcate cohort: use shorter **window=2** with widened thresholds for onboarding segment or exclude entirely via `subscriber_tenure_days` field enriched from BSS.

Network-level outages collapsing usage across regions create fleet-wide phantom decay—correlate with `index=nms OR index=operations` PlannedWork tickets using geo joins on cell site outage maps exported as CSV lookups refreshed hourly.

Operational metrics: precision/recall of decay list vs actual churn in prior quarter should headline executive dashboard—not raw alert counts. Maintain confusion matrix tableau fed by Splunk `outputcsv` nightly.

Advanced union model: unify postpaid decay with prepaid **top-up decay** (companion SPL provided) plus optional handset financing delinquency flags from BSS only if lawful—never merge credit scoring without governance.

Throughput: behavioural searches across full subscriber bases may hit memory joins; summarise weekly per subscriber upstream into summary index with `sicollection` semantics or use `makeresults`-less approach—pre-aggregation via DB warehouse producing narrow Splunk ingest is pragmatic at national scale.

Retention operations feedback loop: ingestion of disposition codes (saved/contacted/opted-out) improves future threshold tuning via closed-loop ROC dashboards—expose `retention_attempt_outcome` as indexed field appended through HEC from CRM bots.
Advanced retention analytics integration and uplift measurement

Holdout cohorts for campaign attribution require surrogate keys aligning Splunk exports to experimentation platforms—document bias when enterprise exclusions slip through stale segmentation lookups.

Threshold calibration can move from static 40/50 to parameters ingested weekly from offline logistic models via CSV lookups referenced in SPL—with static fallback when stale.

Throughput alternative: warehouse pre-computes divergence flags nightly into Splunk while dashboards query JDBC on demand—latency trade-offs belong in architecture decision records.

Operational fatigue governance: export top-decile decay plus random stratified sample so tail behaviour stays qualitatively reviewed without overwhelming retention agents.

Seasonal hysteresis requiring two violating weeks avoids oscillations when roamers rebound after vacations.

SOCAR-style automation closes benign seasonal duplicates only when historical labels approve—expose kill-switch KV respected via macro expansion.
Segment baselines, ethics cadence, and export minimisation

Use `plan_segment` lookups (prepaid wallet, hybrid, enterprise pooled, IoT) carrying distinct decay percentages—global constants mis-rank expected monetary upside of saves.

Quarterly ethics review covers consent coverage drift, suppression of opted-out subscribers, field minimisation on exports, DPIA artefacts, retention purges for ephemeral scores, and audit sampling proving proportionality versus commercial benefit.

Operational integration: summarise weekly into a summary index if raw replay times out—preserve drilldown via subscriber key to raw only for authorised investigations behind break-glass roles.

Campaign measurement attaches holdout cohort flags exported alongside decay rows for clean incremental lift reporting without exaggerating attribution.

Leak response: playbook rotates synthetic export tokens, freezes downstream CRM connectors, verifies HEC pipelines until integrity restores.

Billing distress codes (`soft_collection`, `payment_plan`) optionally suppress churn touches when humane treatment policies already engaged—coordinate with regulatory guidance on vulnerable customers.

Handset launch calendars temporarily widen thresholds when metering artefacts spike post mass device migrations.

Weekly bins anchored with explicit timezone macros per operating company—misaligned anchors fabricate faux decay internationally.

Maintain explainability: regulators and subscriber advocates challenged opaque churn engines—percentage decay from observable usage remains defensible prima facie.
Appendix — feature store hygiene, MLOps boundaries, and campaigning hand-off

If you later enrich decay with ML scores, keep this UC’s Splunk logic as **transparent baseline** referenced in model cards; drift between ML and SPL should be monitored—large divergence indicates model staleness or policy shift.

Campaign hand-off files should carry **versioned schema** (`schema_v=3`) so CRM bots reject mismatched columns rather than silently dropping decay leading to operational blind spots.

For multi-market operators, **macro-expand** `_time` zone per op-co at search time (`eval _time_tz=…`) avoiding duplicated saved searches differing only by locale.

Throughput: Shard heavy `streamstats` by `subscriber_id` prefix using `where cidrmatch("10.0.0.0/8", … )` analogues does not apply—instead use **`bucket` hashing**: `eval shard=subscriber_id % 16` splitting scheduled search into sixteen jobs merged by `savedsearch` chaining if necessary at extreme billion-subscriber hypothetical scale.

Operational analytics: annotate each weekly export batch with **`export_batch_id`** ingested via HEC acknowledgment from CRM for closed-loop SLA measurement.

Privacy testing: quarterly red-team attempting re-identification of masked IDs using auxiliary data—document outcomes in ethics committee minutes.
Model risk, export contracts, and longitudinal bias checks

If marketing later adds ML risk scores alongside decay, require **model cards** stored in Confluence/Git with links from Splunk dashboards; decay SPL remains the regulatory-explainable backbone.

Export contract versioning: CRM ingest jobs must reject files missing `schema_version` field—prevents silent drops of decay columns during agile CRM releases.

Quarterly bias sampling: compare decay flag rates across demographic marketing segments (aggregated, privacy safe) to catch accidental skew disadvantaging protected classes—escalate to ethics committee if statistically significant after multiple testing correction.

Back-pressure planning: if subscriber base doubles, pre-scale SH schedulers or split searches by `subscriber_id` modulus with outputlookup merges—document merge procedure to avoid partial campaign lists.

Operational note: integrate **network outage calendars** to suppress flag weeks where usage drops are externally forced rather than behavioural.
Cohort synthesis between usage-decay and top-up streams

After appending churn-risk searches, summarise with explicit precedence macros (`stats … by subscriber_id`) so prepaid top-up collapse overrides mere data softness when configured that way—or `dedup subscriber_id` after ranking worst-channel decay—to avoid duplicate outbound touches.

Expose `cohort_origin` tags (data/voice/SMS/recharge/multi) so agents tailor offers responsibly.

Reject CRM ingest files lacking `export_schema_version` to prevent silent partial column drops.

Log `export_batch_id` acknowledgements back through HEC for volume reconciliation with lifecycle tools.

BSS coupling, blackout windows, and uplift science

Codify contractual SLAs between Splunk engineering and BSS product teams: mandatory schema change notices, blackout windows around billing releases when behavioural alerts auto-suppress via `bss_release_calendar` lookups, rollback owners when JDBC pulls return suspicious empty sets.

Implement lightweight asserts: nightly `stats count by sourcetype` with static thresholds alerting if extracts vanish silently—prevent false negatives where Splunk dashboards look healthy while warehouse jobs failed.

Scientific uplift: export holdout vs treatment cohort summaries weekly; join outcomes back via `campaign_response` lookups ingested via HEC—Splunk dashboards track incremental saves versus control without overselling causal attribution prematurely.

Synthetic subscribers with scripted churn trajectories anchored in lab indexes prove weekly that decay logic fires predictably—a canary guarding pipeline regressions.

Capacity: when subscriber cardinality doubles, split rolling `streamstats` jobs subscriber-modulo shards merged through `stats` aggregation step—maintains runtime within scheduler windows without compromising detection fidelity.

Operational minimisation drills: periodically attempt re-identification of masked IDs using external synthetic auxiliaries deliberately—document failures as privacy assurance evidence strengthening regulator confidence proportionality arguments.

Operational ethics: downgrade aggressive retention messaging when hardship codes activate—coordinate empathetic exclusions using `subscriber_hardship_lookup` keyed from BSS philanthropic programmes where legal.

Operational integration with finance: annotate decay cohorts with approximate ARPU bands from anonymised lookups—guides commercial prioritisation without exposing individual bills in Splunk.

Close-the-loop dashboards: ingestion of campaign disposition codes verifies whether alerted subscribers actually benefited—prevent vanity metrics masking operational waste.

Budget governance: summarise weekly Splunk search cost per behavioural programme using `_audit`; justify licence burn with uplift ROI quarterly reviews transparently.
Configuration versioning: snapshot `macros.conf`, `savedsearches.conf`, and Alert Manager policies in Git whenever settlement, churn, or voice correlation thresholds change—auditors and regression testing both require reproducible baselines tied to CFO sign-off quarters.Search-time macro hygiene: document owning team, escalation path, last tabletop date, dependent sourcetypes, and wiki deep links directly in macros so rotating engineers cannot resurrect dormant misconfigurations unknowingly.

Step 5 — Troubleshooting
• **Huge false list after metering bug** — compare `data_mb` distribution to prior week; pause alert.
• **Decay null for all** — `streamstats` window too short for sparse events; reduce `span` or require `>=3` samples.
• **SIM-swap false positives** — join device change feed.
• **Seasonal spikes** — add **holiday calendar** suppress.
• **Privacy incident risk** — mask `dest_number` at index time.
• **Search cost** — pre-aggregate weekly in summary index.
• **Contract_end_date near** — merge with **renewal** offers list to avoid redundant saves.
grandmaExplanation: We quietly watch whether people still call, text, and use the internet the way they used to on their phones. When someone fades away for weeks without a normal reason, we can reach out kindly before they walk to another company.
telcoUseCase: Churn analytics — detect declining usage trajectories (data, voice, recharge) in rolling 4-week windows to trigger retention campaigns before number portability requests arrive.
